docs.title: Tour De Blacksburg
docs.text: Welcome to the Tour De Blacksburg. In this guide we will go over global eats around Blacksburg, describing each restaurants menu, price, reviews, location, atmosphere, and personal recommendations.

docs.title: East Asian Eats
docs.text: Blacksburg is home to many East Asian cuisines. This section will highlight Japanese and Chinese restaurants.

docs.title: Kuma Moon
docs.text: Kuma Moon is a japanese restaurant with an incredibly diverse menu. Customers can purchase anything from sapporo ramen to matcha creme cakes.

docs.title: Menu Overview
docs.text:
A key part of Kuma Moon is their abundance of ramen. They have pork, miso, spicy, and vegetarian ramen. As long as you like ramen, there is sure to be an option for you.

 Inferno ramen

If you aren't in the mood for a brothy bowl, they even have "brothless ramen". Other non-ramen options consist of (but not limited to) Donburi, Udon, and Katsu Curry.

This does not encompass everything yummy at Kuma Moon, but their menu will show you further options.

docs.title: Reviews and Pricing
docs.text:
4.5 star rating | $10-20

Many customers' reviews describe Kuma Moon as "cute ambience", "robust menu", and "delicious". Most enjoy the food, with the only negative reviews regarding wait time.

docs.title: Location and Atmosphere
docs.text:
Kuma Moon is located on 725 University City Blvd, near the ABC store and CVS.

⚠️The parking lot is relatively small, with many spots saying "Truist Bank only".

But, parking there after the bank's closed is never an issue. If the parking lot is really full, you can park at the nearby Panera and walk over.

Kuma has a small, cozy atmosphere, typical of small japanese ramen shops. The kitchen is behind the bar, so not only can you see the food being made, but it can get a bit warm too!

There is bar seating along with around 5 tables, along with some outdoor seating, but workers will push tables together for a big party.

docs.title: Personal Recommendation
docs.text: The inferno sapporo ramen is an amazing choice if you are a lover of spicy broth!

docs.title: Green's Grill and Sushi Bar
docs.text: Greens is a Lebanese owned restaurant with a diverse menu, predominantly of sushi.

docs.title: Menu Overview
docs.text:
This diverse menu spans from mediterranean dishes to burgers to fish and chips, but we will focus on their east asian aspects of the menu.

East asian appetizers consist of dumplings, shrimp, and gyoza. They have several different "asian bowls" ranging from Mongolian to Korean Bbq.

Their sushi menu is quite expansive with specialty rolls, handmade rolls, and artisan rolls, and sashimi. Sushi burritos, bento boxes, and poke bowls are another interesting aspect of the menu that differentiates Green's from other sushi restaurants. Overall, Green's is a great restaurant for friends and families with diverse tastes.

docs.title: Reviews and Pricing
docs.text:
3.6 star rating | $10-20

The reviews of Green's tend to speak highly of the sushi. Some saying "beyond delicious" and "big menu, big portions". The low reviews mostly cite the service as being average or below.

Many say the price is justifiable for the large portions.

docs.title: Location and Atmosphere
docs.text:
109 College Ave It is located in the heart of downtown Blacksburg, so street parking is required.

The restaurant is a decent size with lots of tables and some outside seating. Usually incredibly busy, so it can feel crowded. The decor is nice along with evident cleanliness.

docs.title: Personal Recommendations
docs.text: Even though the sushi is wonderful, my personal favorite is the fish and chips. This fish has a slight asian twist being fried in panko bread crumbs and served with asian side sauces. The fries are the perfect texture for fish and chips!

docs.title: Junzi Kitchen
docs.text: Junzi Kitchen is a Chinese restaurant with various kinds of fresh, ingredient forward bowls. This is a fast-casual Chinese restaurant that focuses on reimagined Northern Chinese comfort food.

docs.title: Menu Overview
docs.text: The menu centers around build-your-own grain bowls, noodle bowls, and salads, often featuring ingredients like braised pork, cumin lamb, tofu, seasonal vegetables, and house-made sauces. Signature items include cold noodle bowls and their popular “Beijing-style” dishes.

docs.title: Reviews and Pricing
docs.text:
4.6 star rating | $10-20

Many reviews emphasize the healthiness and variety of textures present in Junzi bowls.

docs.title: Location and Atmosphere
docs.text:
220 Gilbert St Ste 115 Blacksburg

The atmosphere is clean, modern, and minimal, with a focus on quick service and a relaxed, student-friendly environment. Customers can expect efficient service.

docs.title: Personal Recommendation
docs.text: I’d recommend trying a cumin lamb noodle bowl with hand-pulled noodles and chili oil. Tastes are rich, flavorful, and a great blend of spices that define Northern Chinese cuisine.

docs.title: Latin American:
docs.text: Blacksburg does not have a large variety of Latin American restaurants, so the most common spot people go to for Latin American food is definitely El Rodeo.

docs.title: El Rodeo
docs.text: El Rods, as it is commonly known by locals, is a Mexican-American restaurant with two locations in Blacksburg. One is located on North Main St. and the other is on South Main St. The differences between the two are minimal; but the South Main location offers more seating with slightly higher prices on average.

docs.title: Menu Overview
docs.text:
The main dinner menu at El Rods features a large variety of Mexican-American food, including quesadillas, burritos, enchiladas, and other classic Mexican dishes.

Side orders of queso, rice and beans, and a la carte items are available alongside classic Mexican desserts if an entree alone isn’t enough food. El Rodeo is known for their drink menu as well, featuring multiple flavors of margaritas. Flavors include lime, mango, peach, raspberry, and strawberry. Frozen or on the rocks classic margaritas are available alongside a variety of specialty margaritas.

The full menu for the North Main location. The full menu for the South Main location.

docs.title: Reviews and Pricing
docs.text:
North Main - 4.3 star rating | $10-20

South Main - 4.1 star rating | $10-20

Reviews are overall very positive, describing the restaurants as festive, easygoing Mexican-American food.

docs.title: Location and Atmosphere
docs.text:
The atmosphere inside both locations offer great, relaxed vibes. The workers are friendly and casual, with lots of college students and families dining there every night.

The North Main St. location’s address is 623 N Main St. Blacksburg. There is parking on location, directly in front and behind the restaurant.

The South Main Street location’s address is 1540 S Main St Ste 100, Blacksburg.

⚠️This location is a part of a shopping centre, making parking difficult to find during busy times of the day, but there are spaces surrounding the restaurant.

docs.title: Recommendations
docs.text:
The arroz con pollo is my personal recommendation. It is a mixture of rice, cheese, and grilled chicken, and is absolutely delicious.

For drinks, you can’t go wrong with any of the margaritas, but I’d recommend the classic frozen lime marg.

docs.title: Middle Eastern and Mediterranean Food
docs.text: Blacksburg has multiple Middle Eastern or Mediterranean restaurants such as Mezeh, Kabob and More, and Souvlaki, but the best two restaurants of these cuisines combine them both: The Cellar and Italianos.

docs.title: The Cellar
docs.text: The Cellar is a casual Greek-American restaurant located in the heart of downtown Blacksburg, featuring two distinct seating areas - upstairs and downstairs.

docs.title: Menu
docs.text:
The menu at The Cellar offers traditional Greek-American cuisine alongside more traditional American bar food. Options include classic Greek appetizers such as spanakopita, souvlaki, gyro sandwiches, Italian style pastas, pizza, and American dishes like burgers and chicken tenders.

All food choices are the same upstairs and downstairs.

The Cellar is also known for their extensive drink menu, offering different menus for beer depending on if you are seated upstairs or downstairs, with a universal cocktail and shooter menu.

docs.title: Reviews and Pricing
docs.text:
4.4 star rating | $10-20

Customer reviews highlight the high quality food, great atmosphere, and friendly staff.

docs.title: Atmosphere and Location
docs.text:
The Cellar offers a very casual atmosphere regardless if you sit upstairs or downstairs. However, the upstairs is best for families or for those searching for a more standard dining experience, with the downstairs featuring dark bar lighting best for college students.

Located at 302 N Main St. Blacksburg, The Cellar has a parking lot directly behind it that requires payment during weekdays and is free at night and during the weekend.

docs.title: Recommendations
docs.text: The Cellar’s menu is truly fantastic, with many great choices, but the pizzas are a personal favorite, as you can customize them, choosing up to three toppings for no additional charge.

docs.title: Italianos
docs.text: Italianos is a casual Italian and Middle Eastern restaurant that offers quick and easy Italian and Halal food with great portions.

docs.title: Menu
docs.text:
The menu at Italianos offers a wide range of Mediterranean and halal dishes. They specialize in making standard, specialty, and thick crust pizzas, salads, calzones, strombolis, burgers, sandwiches, pasta, and halal gyros and rice plates.

Standard pizzeria sides are available such as breadsticks and mozzarella sticks; and there is a small dessert section featuring cheesecake, cannolis, and baklava.

docs.title: Reviews and Pricing
docs.text:
4.1 star rating | $10-20

Reviews highlight the great affordability and high portions of the delicious pizzas, pastas, and halal dishes.

docs.title: Atmosphere and Location
docs.text:
The interior of the restaurant is incredibly small, offering only a few areas to sit and eat, which makes Italianos a great carryout and delivery restaurant - especially considering the quick service time.

The restaurant is located at 610 N Main St. Blacksburg, right next to the downtown Waffle House. Parking is available directly in front of the restaurant.

docs.title: Recommendations
docs.text: The halal gyro with lamb and chicken is one of the most popular dishes - and my personal recommendation. The meat is always cooked well and paired with delicious sauces and a side of fries.

docs.title: South Asian Cuisine
docs.text: Blacksburg has several South and Southeast Asian restaurants to dine at. From India, to Nepal, to Thailand, there's an option for everyone!

docs.title: Namaste Kitchen
docs.text: Namaste's cuisine is mixed: They offer both Indian and Nepali dishes.

docs.title: Menu Overview
docs.text:
Namaste serves a variety of entrees, including tandoori, biryani, chow mein, fried rice, and goat, lamb, and chicken-based meals. You can find their full menu by clicking the top left button on their website.

If you don't eat meat, you're in luck. Namaste also offers many options for vegetarians, including dedicated lists of vegetarian appetizers, entrees, and momos, which are bite-sized dumplings that originate in Tibet.

docs.title: Reviews and Pricing
docs.text:
Namaste has a Google rating of 4.6 stars. | $20-30

⚠️A filling meal will likely be on the higher end.

docs.title: Location and Atmosphere
docs.text:
This restaurant is located downtown at 239 North Main Street. You'll find it on the corner just before the turn onto College Avenue by the Squire's parking lot.

Inside, the restaurant is cozy and well decorated. It can get a bit loud and crowded when full, but the staff has been described as kind and attentive to their customers.

⚠️Service can get slow during peak hours. Make sure to check the restaurant's activity on Google.

docs.title: Personal Recommendation
docs.text: A bit of a predictable answer, but the Chicken Tikka Masala here is really good.

docs.title: Hamro Kitchen
docs.text: Another kitchen for your consideration! Hamro is a Nepali restaurant.

docs.title: Menu Overview
docs.text: Hamro offers a wide range of Nepali cuisine. You can find the full menu here. They have biryani, momo, tandoori, and dedicated vegetarian options. Meat entrees come in chicken, lamb, mutton, and seafood, served with basmati rice.

docs.title: Reviews and Pricing
docs.text: Hamro has a Google rating of 4.6 stars. | $10-20

docs.title: Location and Atmosphere
docs.text: You can find Hamro in the southern part of downtown Blacksburg at 403 Draper Road NW. It's just off South Main Street by the Blacksburg Public Library.

docs.title: Personal Recommendation
docs.text: Hamro’s paneer dishes are really good. I’d recommend getting either the mutter paneer or the paneer tikka masala.

docs.title: Cafe Mekong
docs.text: Cafe Mekong is a Thai and Vietnamese restaurant.

docs.title: Menu Overview
docs.text:
Cafe Mekong offers Thai curries, noodles like pad thai and pad siew, fried rice entrees, and several Vietnamese dishes including pho. They also have a few options from other Asian countries like Korea, China, and Singapore. You’ll find them in their “Special Entrees” section. You can find their full menu here.

At the end of the menu, you can find vegan versions of some of their most popular noodle and rice dishes.

docs.title: Reviews and Pricing
docs.text: This cafe has a Google rating of 4.5 stars. | $10-20

docs.title: Location and Atmosphere
docs.text:
Cafe Mekong is located on 1405 South Main Street, across the street from Kroger. You'll find it right next to Oasis.

This restaurant is small, but it has plenty of seating and a cozy feel. Since it's located in a plaza with a grocery store, there's also plenty of parking right out front. Service tends to be pretty quick and many reviewers have mentioned that the staff there is helpful, kind, and provides good customer service.

docs.title: Personal Recommendation
docs.text: I love the panang curry from here. I usually get mine with tofu, but you can choose any of their other protein options.
